name: Restaurant & Catering
description: Table bookings, catering enquiries and private events — Bookings and Menu Packages objects that track covers, dietary needs and the date the diary is held.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a restaurant that also sells private dining, venue hire and off-site catering. Contacts are diners, regulars, event organisers and corporate bookers; companies are the businesses, agencies and hotels that book on their behalf. A deal is one event or catering enquiry worked from first contact to a confirmed, deposit-paid booking in the diary — ordinary table reservations live on the Booking object, not the pipeline. Never invent availability, per-head prices, menu contents or allergen information: state only what is recorded on the booking or the menu package, and route any allergy question to the kitchen.

automation_rules.name: Call an enquiry back the same day
automation_rules.description: Event enquiries go out to three venues at once and the diary date is gone within days — this books the callback the moment the contact is created.

params.title: Call {{contact.first_name}} back — confirm the date, covers, service and any dietary or allergen requirements
New enquiry logged at {{org.name}}
params.body: Check the diary before promising anything. Hold the date as Provisional on a booking record, set Guest Type, and put every allergy in Dietary & Allergen Notes in the guest's own words — never paraphrase an allergy.

automation_rules.name: Confirm the details of a booking
automation_rules.description: Fires the moment a deal reaches Confirmed in Diary — the point where work is won and something has to happen next.

params.title: Confirm final covers, dietary needs and timings with {{contact.first_name}}

kb_templates.company:
## About us

[Restaurant name] in [town/city] — [cuisine], [number] covers, open [days and services]. Replace this with who you are and how long you have been open.

## Spaces

Main dining room [covers], private room [covers], terrace [covers], full venue hire [covers standing / seated].

## Contact

Reservations line, events email, and who covers events on the days the manager is off.
kb_templates.products:
## Menus

- À la carte — changes [frequency]
- Set lunch — [price] for [courses]
- Tasting menu — [price], [courses], whole table only

## Events and catering

Private dining from [minimum covers] at [price] per head. Full venue hire minimum spend [amount], varies by day. Off-site catering within [distance].

## The essentials

Corkage [amount], service charge [%], children's menu [yes/no], accessible entrance [details].
kb_templates.playbook:
## Taking an event enquiry

1. Date first — check the diary and say honestly whether it is free before discussing anything else.
2. Covers, and whether the number is firm or a guess.
3. Service: seated lunch or dinner, canapés, or full venue hire.
4. Budget per head, asked plainly. It saves everyone a tasting.
5. Dietary requirements and allergies, recorded in the guest's own words.

## Allergies

Never improvise. Write it down verbatim, pass it to the kitchen, and confirm back in writing what the kitchen has agreed. Cross-contamination questions go to the head chef, not to the front of house.
kb_templates.process:
## From enquiry to a date in the diary

1. **Enquiry** — capture the date, the covers and the occasion. Anything else can wait.
2. **Availability Checked** — diary checked and the date held as Provisional for [number] days.
3. **Menu & Quote Sent** — package, per-head price, minimum spend and deposit terms in writing.
4. **Site Visit or Tasting** — they have seen the room or tasted the menu. Follow up within 48 hours.
5. **Deposit Invoiced** — invoice out. The provisional hold expires on [date] and they must be told that.
6. **Confirmed in Diary** — deposit received, booking set to Confirmed, final numbers and dietaries due [number] days before.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Who we lose to

- **Hotels** — one venue for the dinner and the beds, and a dedicated events team. We win on food and on a room that feels like somewhere, not a function suite.
- **Dedicated event venues** — bigger capacity and their own AV. We win on the kitchen and on a lower minimum spend.
- **The restaurant round the corner** — usually price. Do not chase it; win on the private room and on service.

Replace these with the venues you actually lose to, and the honest answer to each.
